Replication: Active-active across 3 regions. Policy Enforcement: 200 vCPUs, 800 GB RAM. Must process 100K policy evaluations/second at peak. BOSS Scoring Engine: 100 vCPUs, 400 GB RAM, GPU acceleration for ML-based recalibration. Flight Recorder: 100 vCPUs, 200 GB RAM, append-only high-throughput storage (50K writes/second). Intent Processing: 100 vCPUs, 200 GB RAM for Intent Interpretation + Trust Gateway agents. Total ADAM Governance: ~1,000 vCPUs, 5.6 TB RAM across all regions.
compute.agent_mesh: Domain Governor Agents (5 Governer Agents): 50 vCPUs each, 200 GB RAM each (GPU for reasoning). 250 vCPUs total. Orchestration Agents (4): 100 vCPUs each, 400 GB RAM each. 400 vCPUs total. Corporate Work Groups (39 agents): Average 20 vCPUs, 80 GB RAM each. 780 vCPUs total. AI-Centric Work Groups (23 agents): Average 30 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM each (more compute for ML). 690 vCPUs total. Human Interface Agents (3): 50 vCPUs each, 200 GB RAM each. 150 vCPUs total. Digital Twin Agents (4): 200 vCPUs each, 800 GB RAM each, GPU for simulation. 800 vCPUs total. Meta-Governance Agents (3): 30 vCPUs each, 120 GB RAM each. 90 vCPUs total. Total Agent Mesh: ~3,160 vCPUs, 14.2 TB RAM. GPU: 2,000 inference GPUs (A100 equivalent) for ML models + Digital Twin simulations.
compute.auto_scaling: CORE Engine: Fixed size (always-on, no scale-down). Agent Mesh: Horizontal auto-scale based on intent volume. Scale-up trigger: Intent queue depth >100 or agent response latency P95 >500ms. Scale-up speed: New agent instances ready in <90 seconds. Min instances: 3 per agent (HA). Max instances: 50x for customer-facing agents during peak. Scale-down: 10-minute cooldown, minimum 5-minute sustained low utilization. Pre-scale events: Scheduled 2x capacity expansion before major content launches, 3x for live events.

deployment_params.storage: CORE Graph (Graph Database): 50 TB hot storage, optimized for traversal queries. Azure Cosmos DB Gremlin API (primary) / AWS Neptune (secondary). Multi-region write enabled. Flight Recorder: 500 TB per year growing. Append-only, immutable. Azure Blob Storage (primary) / AWS S3 (secondary). Glacier/Archive after 1 year. Intent Object Store: 10 TB. Low-latency, versioned. Cosmos DB (primary). BOSS Score History: 20 TB. Time-series optimized. Azure Data Explorer / AWS Timestream. Digital Twin State: 100 TB. In-memory compute + SSD spillover. Model Artifacts: 50 TB. Versioned model registry. Azure ML / AWS SageMaker. Encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, customer-managed keys for all PII and governance data.
deployment_params.networking: Networking: Inter-region backbone 10 Gbps (ExpressRoute + Direct Connect). Intra-region: 100 GbE fabric. All services via private endpoints. No public internet exposure for governance components. Security: Azure AD (Entra ID) as primary IdP, federated to AWS SSO. MFA enforced. JIT access for production (15-minute windows). Automated threat detection (Sentinel + GuardDuty). 24/7 SOC. DR: Governance planes RPO = 0 (synchronous replication). Agent mesh RPO <15 minutes. RTO: Governance <5 minutes (automated failover). Agent mesh <15 minutes. Failover to Azure Stack (manual trigger by CISO): Critical services only, within 15 minutes. Monthly automated failover test to AWS. Quarterly Azure Stack drill.

on_premises.config: Azure Stack is the nuclear option — activated only when both Azure and AWS are simultaneously unavailable, or for data residency isolation requirements. Services on Azure Stack: CORE Engine (read-only graph replica), Policy Enforcement (cached policies), Authentication/session management, Flight Recorder (local write, sync on reconnection), BOSS Scoring (cached weights), Intent processing (emergency only). NOT on Azure Stack: Full Agent Mesh (reduced to 5 Governer Agents + 4 Orchestrators only), Digital Twins (offline), Innovation agents (offline), Full analytics (offline). Capacity: 30% of production traffic. 3 locations: 24-node clusters each.
on_premises.sync_strategy: Continuous sync: CORE Graph delta replication to Azure Stack (every 5 minutes). Top-priority doctrine versions cached locally. Policy snapshots refreshed hourly. Disconnected mode: Azure Stack can operate for 72 hours without cloud connectivity using cached credentials, local DNS, and pre-provisioned certificates. Failback: Always manual. Requires data consistency verification. 4-hour minimum validation window. Flight Recorder entries from Azure Stack merge into primary store with conflict detection. ADAM does not self-failback — the CISO must explicitly authorize return to cloud.

cross_cloud.management: Management: Azure Arc as primary control plane across all three environments. GitOps (Flux/ArgoCD) with environment-specific overlays. IaC: Terraform for cross-cloud, Bicep for Azure-native, CloudFormation for AWS-native. Observability: Grafana Cloud unified dashboards. OpenTelemetry distributed tracing across all clouds. PagerDuty as single alert destination. Deployment pipeline: Lint → Plan → Security scan → Cost estimate → Apply to dev → Integration tests → Staging → Approval gate → Production (blue-green). Drift detection: Daily Terraform plan comparison. Monthly automated failover test.
cross_cloud.ai_services: Azure: Azure Deployment Environments for standardized ADAM templates. Azure Advisor for optimization. Azure Copilot for infrastructure queries. AWS: AWS Application Composer for warm-standby architecture. AWS Compute Optimizer for right-sizing. Cross-cloud: Terraform Cloud with Sentinel policies for governance. Pulumi AI for infrastructure code generation. AI-driven predictive auto-scaling (trained on ADAM’s own operational data). AI cost anomaly detection. AI-assisted incident remediation (Azure AI Ops, AWS DevOps Guru). Target: Feed this entire questionnaire into Azure/AWS AI buildout services to auto-generate the complete Terraform, Bicep, and CloudFormation templates for ADAM’s infrastructure.

deployment_params.sovereignty_boundary: MUST be jurisdictionally controlled: CORE Engine + CORE Graph (doctrine is the crown jewel), Policy Enforcement Orchestrator, Cryptographic Authorization Vault, Flight Recorder (evidence store), Intent Object storage, BOSS scoring engine, Human Interface agents (Trust Gateway, Explain-Back). CAN run on commercial cloud: Agentic Work Groups (execution layer), Digital Twins (simulation), Data pipelines and analytics, Content delivery and CDN, Customer-facing application layer. Separation: Control planes are sovereign; data planes can be distributed. If the governance layer is controlled by the enterprise, sovereignty is maintained regardless of where compute runs.
deployment_params.data_residency: EU citizen data: Azure West Europe (primary), AWS eu-west-1 (secondary), Azure Stack Amsterdam (failover). US data: Azure East US (primary), AWS us-east-1 (secondary), Azure Stack Virginia (failover). APAC data: Azure Southeast Asia (primary), AWS ap-northeast-1 (secondary), Azure Stack Tokyo (failover). CORE Graph: Federated across regions with sovereignty-compliant data masking — each region has a full replica but PII is masked to local-only access. Flight Recorder: Regional primary, cross-region encrypted backup (metadata only, not PII).

governance.mission: To entertain the world — giving every person on earth access to the stories, ideas, and experiences that move them.
governance.vision: To become the world’s leading entertainment platform where any human, in any language, on any device, can find a story that changes their perspective.
principles: Creator Freedom — We never override creative vision for commercial pressure.
Customer Trust as Foundation — Every decision starts with ‘does this protect or enhance the viewer’s trust?’
Evidence Over Opinion — Data informs, evidence proves, opinion yields.
Radical Transparency Internally — Information flows freely; hoarding information is a fireable offense.
Compliance Is Not Optional — Regulatory obligations are mandates, never soft constraints.
sacred_boundaries: Never sell or share individual viewing data to third parties
Never produce content that exploits minors
Never misrepresent AI-generated content as human-created without disclosure
Never accept advertising that conflicts with content editorial independence
Never silently resolve a doctrine conflict — always escalate to human directors
governance.directors: CEO (Primary Accountability) — Owns overall enterprise intent, final arbiter for irreconcilable doctrine conflicts, sole authority for mission/vision changes. CFO (Financial Stewardship) — Owns financial doctrine, spending thresholds, capital allocation, autonomy budget caps. Legal Director — Owns regulatory compliance doctrine, jurisdictional rules, legal personality boundary enforcement. Market Director — Owns external posture, brand doctrine, competitive strategy constraints. CISO — Owns security doctrine, trust boundaries, resilience posture, incident response thresholds.
governance.delegation_model: Delegation is explicit (defined as CORE configuration), scoped (bounded by domain, risk threshold, and financial cap), revocable (any director can throttle autonomy in their domain to zero within seconds), and auditable (every delegation creates a Flight Recorder entry). CEO delegates operational execution up to $5M/transaction. CFO delegates routine financial operations up to $500K without escalation. Legal Director delegates compliance monitoring but requires escalation for any novel regulatory interpretation. All delegations are versioned and subject to quarterly review.
values_trade_offs: Innovation risk > operational safety (bounded by autonomy budget). These priority orderings become deterministic resolution logic for the CORE Engine when agents encounter competing signals.

boss_config.recalibration_cadence: Quarterly: Full recalibration using prior quarter’s operational data (exceptions, near-misses, false positives, override patterns). Monthly: Threshold sensitivity check — are escalation volumes appropriate? (Target: 5-15% of actions generate exceptions.) Real-time: If false positive rate exceeds 25% in any dimension for 7 consecutive days, trigger automatic threshold review. Annual: Board-level review of BOSS model integrity with external validation.
boss_config.exception_packets: Every exception packet contains: 1) The original Intent Object (full schema). 2) The proposed action and execution plan. 3) Per-dimension BOSS scores with policy provenance. 4) The specific dimension(s) that triggered escalation. 5) Predicted impact analysis (financial, regulatory, reputational). 6) Alternative approaches with their BOSS scores. 7) Recommended decision with confidence level. 8) Time sensitivity (how long can this wait?). 9) Link to related historical exceptions for pattern context. 10) One-click approve/reject/modify interface.
boss_config.target_exception_volume: Target: 5-15 exceptions per director per day across all domains. Warning threshold: >20 exceptions/day for any single director suggests thresholds are too conservative or the domain needs additional delegation capacity. Critical: >30/day triggers automatic threshold relaxation for low-risk exception categories + alert to CEO. ADAM tracks escalation frequency, resolution time, and override patterns to continuously optimize this balance.
boss_config.feedback_loop: Every resolved exception is stored in the Flight Recorder with: the original exception, the human decision, the reasoning (if provided), and the outcome. Monthly analysis: Exception patterns are clustered by type. If a class of exceptions is approved >90% of the time without modification, the system proposes a threshold adjustment to auto-approve similar future cases. If a class is rejected >50%, the system proposes a tighter constraint. Proposed doctrine changes are presented to the relevant director as a structured proposal — ADAM never self-amends doctrine.

domain_governors.8.1.1: Capital allocation framework: 36% content, 12% engineering, 9% infrastructure, 7.5% marketing, 4.5% G&A, remaining to growth initiatives. Spending tiers: Tier 1 (<$100K) auto-approved, Tier 2 ($100K-$500K) Financial Governer Agent review, Tier 3 ($500K-$5M) CFO escalation, Tier 4 (>$5M) CEO + CFO + board notification. Trade-off rules: Efficiency vs. growth — prefer growth when monthly burn <85% of budget; prefer efficiency when >85%. Investment horizon: Discount future value at 12% for content investments, 8% for infrastructure.
domain_governors.8.1.2: Jurisdictional precedence: Apply the most restrictive applicable regulation (e.g., GDPR applies globally for EU citizen data regardless of processing location). Novel interpretation: Any regulatory question without clear precedent escalates to Legal Director — ADAM does not interpret ambiguous law. Compliance posture: Proactive — agents should anticipate regulatory changes and prepare rather than react. Regulatory monitoring: Continuous scan of regulatory updates in all 195 operating jurisdictions. Compliance certification: Maintain SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, PCI-DSS Level 1.
domain_governors.8.1.3: Risk appetite: Moderate-aggressive for content investment, conservative for financial operations, zero-tolerance for regulatory compliance and data security. Risk categories: Strategic (market shifts), Operational (outages), Financial (FX, subscriber loss), Legal/Regulatory, Reputational, Technology (security breaches), AI-specific (model drift, bias). Dynamic adjustment: If any risk dimension exceeds 70 on BOSS scale for >10% of actions in a 24-hour period, automatically reduce autonomy budget by 30% in that domain and alert the relevant director.
domain_governors.8.1.4: External coherence: All public-facing actions must align with declared brand posture (Section 2.2.1). Pricing vs. reputation: Never raise prices >$3/month in any single change without 60-day customer notice. Competitive response: Never publicly disparage competitors. React to competitive launches with quality messaging, not attack messaging. Market regime shift detection: Subscriber growth <1% in any major market for 2 consecutive months triggers strategy review escalation to Market Director + CEO.
domain_governors.8.1.5: Strategy translation: Annual strategy is decomposed into quarterly executable constraints with measurable KRs. Each KR maps to one or more Domain Governor agents. CORE Graph alignment: Every proposed initiative is evaluated against all four CORE dimensions (Culture, Objectives, Rules, Expectations). Misalignment >2 dimensions = auto-reject with arbitration. Innovation boundary: ADAM may propose innovations through the Innovation Portfolio Agent. Innovations that require >$1M investment or affect >10% of the user base require human director approval. Simulation must show positive expected value on Digital Twin before approval request.

work_groups.corporate: Financial Stewardship: Transaction processing, reconciliation, budgeting/forecasting, capital allocation, audit preparation, economic efficiency optimization. Legal & Regulatory: Contract lifecycle management, regulatory interpretation, compliance monitoring, legal risk analysis, jurisdictional logic. Market Interface: Customer interaction (contracts, SLAs, pricing), partner coordination, market intelligence, demand signal interpretation, reputation monitoring. Operational Continuity: Cross-system dependency awareness, failure recovery, business continuity, resilience testing, catastrophic scenario handling. [Continue for all 8 domains]
work_groups.ai_centric: Autonomy Governance: Manage autonomy budgets, authority boundaries, escalation logic. Continuous: Track escalation frequency and false positive rates. Audit & Evidence: Collect, correlate, store action evidence. Continuous: Validate evidence completeness, run internal audit simulations. Ethics & Trust: Monitor decisions for bias, fairness, ethical alignment. Continuous: Statistical analysis of decision patterns across demographics. CORE Graph: Maintain CORE Graph integrity, detect drift. Continuous: Alignment scoring between actions and declared values. Innovation: Manage experiment pipeline, simulation, safe rollouts. Continuous: Track experiment results, kill underperforming experiments.

digital_twins.definitions: Enterprise Twin: Models ADAM’s own structure — agents, dependencies, capabilities, current state, constraint surfaces, failure modes. ‘What is true right now?’ Operational Twin: Models execution flows and performance — simulates load, failure, and recovery scenarios. Tests changes before execution. Feeds Orchestration and Risk layers. Economic Twin: Models money as a system — simulates pricing, cost, capital allocation. Stress-tests financial decisions. Interfaces with Financial Governer Agent. Risk & Compliance Twin: Predicts future violations — simulates regulatory outcomes, identifies latent legal exposure, provides early-warning signals.
digital_twins.simulation_requirements: Enterprise Twin: Agent mesh reconfiguration, authority boundary changes, new agent deployment. Latency: <30 seconds for simple scenarios, <5 minutes for full mesh simulation. Operational Twin: Load simulation (10x peak), cascading failure scenarios, regional failover. Latency: <2 minutes for operational scenarios. Economic Twin: Pricing change impact (+/- 3 pricing scenarios), budget reallocation, revenue projection (3 quarters forward). Latency: <5 minutes for financial modeling. Risk & Compliance Twin: New regulation impact assessment, jurisdiction expansion risk, cumulative liability exposure. Latency: <10 minutes for regulatory simulation. All simulations must produce explainable results that can be presented in exception packets.

infrastructure.resilience_tiers: Tier 0 (Normal): Full autonomy. Standard BOSS thresholds. All agents operational. Tier 1 (Degraded): 1+ non-critical agents offline. Autonomy budget reduced 20%. Enhanced logging. Human notification. Tier 2 (Impaired): Critical service degradation. Autonomy budget reduced 50%. Only essential agents execute. Directors notified within 15 minutes. Tier 3 (Crisis): Major outage or security incident. Autonomy budget at minimum (mandates only). All non-essential execution halted. CISO + CEO actively directing. Tier 4 (Catastrophic): Azure Stack failover activated. ADAM operates in survival mode. Human directors physically present. Governance evidence capture prioritized above execution.
infrastructure.idempotency_requirements: MUST be idempotent: All financial transactions, content publication, rights verification, customer communications, data exports, regulatory filings, contract modifications. Non-idempotent actions must be wrapped in compensating controls: If non-idempotent action fails mid-execution, a compensating action reverses the partial effect. Non-idempotent actions automatically receive +15 penalty on BOSS score, pushing them toward human review. Acceptable non-idempotent: One-time hardware provisioning (compensated by deprovisioning), unique event generation (captured in Flight Recorder for manual resolution).
infrastructure.security_posture: Agent identity: Every agent has a unique cryptographic identity. No agent can impersonate another. Access: Agents have scoped permissions matching their domain (principle of least privilege). Financial agents cannot access content systems. Content agents cannot access financial systems. Cross-domain access requires Orchestration Plane mediation. Behavior: Agent behavior is bounded by policy, not by trust in the agent’s model. Prompt manipulation resistance: Prompts influence reasoning but never override execution constraints. Anomaly detection: Statistical monitoring of agent request patterns. Unusual patterns trigger security exception. Agent promotion/demotion: Performance-based. Unreliable agents get reduced authority automatically.
infrastructure.threat_model: Primary threats: 1) Prompt injection attempting to bypass content rights checks. 2) Data poisoning of recommendation models to promote specific content. 3) Unauthorized financial transaction attempts via compromised agent identity. 4) Regulatory evasion attempts routed through low-regulation jurisdictions. 5) Insider threat via director account compromise. Mitigations: 1) Rights checks are in execution layer, not reasoning layer. 2) Model training data provenance verification. 3) Financial agents require multi-factor authorization proof from Vault. 4) Jurisdictional routing locked by Regulatory Subgraph (not overridable by reasoning). 5) Director actions require hardware MFA + Trust Gateway consent recording.
